column8: root cause analysis in close notes: 796","This segment represents 845 SAP Cloud ALM incidents monitoring ERP SAP Core systems with consistently poor log quality (average score 51.06). All incidents lack fundamental diagnostic context including impacted SAP components, metric thresholds, correlation IDs, and alert payloads. The incidents primarily involve ABAP license expiration and short dumps, but the automated alerts from Cloud ALM provide minimal actionable data. Work notes show manual investigation and coordination with BASIS/ABAP teams, while close notes contain only generic auto-closure text. The extremely high average MTTR (1699 minutes / 28 hours) reflects the significant manual effort required to gather context that should be present in initial alerts.","No identification of specific SAP component, instance ID, or client causing the alert

column1: }","These logging improvements are expected to reduce MTTR by 40-60% by eliminating manual lookup of system topology, metric baselines, and Cloud ALM situation details. Structured metric values and thresholds enable immediate severity assessment, while correlation IDs and direct URLs reduce navigation time from 3-5 minutes to under 30 seconds. Mandatory root cause documentation builds a searchable knowledge base for similar incidents, enabling pattern recognition and eventual auto-remediation of recurring ABAP performance issues."
